reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for Toxoplasmosis in humans, and no candidates are in late-stage clinical trials that could realistically be approved by March 2026. While a veterinary vaccine (Toxovax) exists for sheep, the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page focuses on humans and currently lists "No" for the Toxoplasmosis vaccine status. For the table to list "Yes" on any of the resolution dates, it would require either an unprecedented rapid approval of a human vaccine, or an erroneous Wikipedia edit (e.g., vandalism or a misapplied inclusion of the animal vaccine) that survives precisely on the resolution date. Such uncorrected modifications on highly visible tables in Wikipedia are relatively rare and usually reverted quickly. Consequently, the probability remains very low, with a slight increase over longer horizons to account for the marginally higher chance of unexpected events or prolonged Wikipedia edit disputes over time.
